Description: Comprehensive debugging suite for Flutter with widget inspector performance timeline memory and network profiling
Command: flutter pub global activate devtools\nflutter run --debug\n# Open DevTools from VS Code / terminal URL
When To Use: Always during development; for debugging rendering issues memory leaks and network calls
Output: Widget tree inspector; Performance timeline; Memory heap; Network requests; CPU profiler

Description: Static analysis tool that checks Dart code for errors warnings and lint rule violations
Command:
flutter analyze
flutter analyze --fatal-infos --fatal-warnings

# In CI
flutter analyze --no-pub || exit 1
When To Use: Before every commit; in CI pipeline; to enforce code quality standards
Output: List of warnings errors and info messages with file locations and descriptions

Description: Automatically applies fixes for deprecated APIs and common issues
Command: dart fix --dry-run  # Preview changes\ndart fix --apply    # Apply all fixes\ndart fix --apply --code=unnecessary_const
When To Use: After upgrading Flutter/Dart version; to fix deprecation warnings; to apply bulk lint fixes
Output: Applied fixes summary showing files changed and fixes applied

Description: Runs code generators for freezed json_serializable injectable and other codegen packages
Command:
# One-time build
dart run build_runner build --delete-conflicting-outputs

# Watch mode
dart run build_runner watch --delete-conflicting-outputs

# Clean and rebuild
dart run build_runner clean && dart run build_runner build
When To Use: After changing models with @freezed @JsonSerializable; after adding @injectable classes
Output: Generated .g.dart and .freezed.dart files with serialization and freezed implementations

Description: Automates building testing and releasing iOS and Android apps including signing and store uploads
Command:
# Install
brew install fastlane

# iOS beta
cd ios && fastlane beta

# Android release
cd android && fastlane deploy

# Match (iOS signing)
fastlane match development
fastlane match appstore
When To Use: For automated releases; CI/CD pipeline; managing iOS signing certificates; beta distribution
Output: Built and signed IPA/APK; uploaded to App Store Connect / Play Console; distributed to testers

Description: Firebase toolchain for crash reporting analytics push notifications remote config and more
Command:
# Install
npm install -g firebase-tools

# Initialize
firebase init

# Deploy functions
firebase deploy --only functions

# Crashlytics symbols
firebase crashlytics:symbols:upload
When To Use: For crash reporting setup; for sending push notifications; for A/B testing with Remote Config
Output: Firebase project configuration; deployed cloud functions; crash reports; analytics dashboards

Description: Desktop debugging tool for React Native and native mobile apps with plugin support
Command:
# React Native
npm install react-native-flipper

# View network requests, databases, shared preferences
# Layout inspector for React Native
When To Use: For React Native development; for inspecting network calls databases and layout
Output: Network request/response inspector; database browser; shared preferences editor; layout hierarchy

Description: Built-in Android Studio profilers for CPU memory network and energy consumption analysis
Command:
# Open Android Studio > View > Tool Windows > Profiler
# Or: Profile app (run button dropdown)
# CPU: Record method trace
# Memory: Heap dump and allocation tracking
When To Use: For Android-specific performance optimization; memory leak detection; energy profiling
Output: CPU method traces; memory allocation timelines; network activity; energy consumption graphs

Description: Apple's profiling toolkit for memory leaks CPU usage allocations and more on iOS
Command: # From Xcode: Product > Profile (Cmd+I)\n# Choose template: Time Profiler / Leaks / Allocations\n# Record and analyze
When To Use: For iOS-specific performance; memory leak detection; CPU hotspot analysis
Output: Time profiler flamegraphs; leaked object details; allocation history; energy impact

Description: Built-in test runner for unit widget and integration tests with coverage support
Command:
# Run all tests
flutter test

# With coverage
flutter test --coverage

# Specific file
flutter test test/unit/auth_test.dart

# Update goldens
flutter test --update-goldens
When To Use: For running all test types; for CI pipelines; for coverage reports
Output: Test results (pass/fail); coverage report in coverage/lcov.info; golden file updates

Description: CLI tool for generating Flutter projects packages and features with best practices baked in
Command:
# Install
dart pub global activate very_good_cli

# Create new project
very_good create flutter_app my_app --desc 'My awesome app'

# Create package
very_good create flutter_package my_package
When To Use: For starting new projects with best practices; for consistent project structure across team
Output: Scaffolded project with testing CI linting and package structure following best practices

Description: Manages multiple Flutter SDK versions per project
Command:
# Install
brew tap leoafarias/fvm && brew install fvm

# Use specific version
fvm install 3.16.0
fvm use 3.16.0

# Project-specific
fvm use stable
fvm flutter run
When To Use: When working on multiple projects with different Flutter versions; for CI reproducibility
Output: Symlinked Flutter SDK in .fvm/; .fvmrc for project version; consistent versions across team

Description: Template-based code generation for creating features screens and components from custom templates
Command:
# Install
dart pub global activate mason_cli

# Create a brick
mason init
mason make feature --name auth

# Use community bricks
mason add bloc_feature
mason make bloc_feature --name products
When To Use: For generating feature boilerplate consistently; for team-wide code templates
Output: Generated files from template with variables replaced: bloc events states screens and tests

Description: Cloud CI/CD platforms specialized for mobile app building testing and publishing
Command:
# Codemagic (codemagic.yaml)
workflows:
  ios-release:
    scripts:
      - flutter test
      - flutter build ipa
    publishing:
      app_store_connect: ...

# Bitrise - configure via web UI or bitrise.yml
When To Use: For automated builds on every push; for release pipelines; when local builds are not feasible
Output: Built artifacts (IPA/APK/AAB); test results; published to stores; build notifications

Description: HTTP proxy tools for intercepting and debugging network traffic from mobile apps
Command:
# Proxyman (macOS)
brew install --cask proxyman
# Configure device proxy to Mac IP:9090
# Install Proxyman CA certificate on device

# View all HTTP/HTTPS traffic
When To Use: For debugging API requests/responses; for testing edge cases with breakpoints; for mock responses
Output: All HTTP/HTTPS traffic captured with request/response details; ability to modify and replay
